subject: Physics
q: A body of mass m moves along a circular path of radius r with uniform speed v. The force acting on it has magnitude $\frac{mv^2}{r}$ and always acts towards the centre of the circle. How much work is done by this force when the body covers half of the circular path?

q: Two springs have their force constant as $k_1$ and $k_2$ ($k_1 > k_2$). When they are stretched individually by the same constant force up to equilibrium -
question: Two springs have their force constant as $k_1$ and $k_2$ ($k_1 > k_2$). When they are stretched individually by the same constant force up to equilibrium -
options: No work is done by this force in case of both the springs
Equal work is done by this force in case of both the springs
More work is done by this force in case of second spring
More work is done by this force in case of first spring

subject: Physics
q: A force varying with position, $F=7-2 x+3 x^2$ newton, acts on a small object of mass 2 kg and moves it from $x=0$ to $x=5 \mathrm{~m}$. If the work done, in joules, is $15 x$, find the value of $x$.

subject: Physics
q: The force on a particle depends on its displacement $\mathbf{x}$ according to $\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{a x}-\mathbf{b} \mathbf{x}^{\mathbf{2}}$. Here $\mathbf{a}=\mathbf{1} \mathbf{N} / \mathbf{m}$ and $b=1 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. If the work done by this force during the first one meter ( $F$ is in newton; $x$ is in meters) is $\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}$ Joule, find the value of x.
question: The force on a particle depends on its displacement $\mathbf{x}$ according to $\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{a x}-\mathbf{b} \mathbf{x}^{\mathbf{2}}$. Here $\mathbf{a}=\mathbf{1} \mathbf{N} / \mathbf{m}$ and $b=1 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. If the work done by this force during the first one meter ( $F$ is in newton; $x$ is in meters) is $\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}$ Joule, find the value of x.

subject: Physics
q: A particle is shifted from the position $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}_{\mathbf{1}}=\mathbf{3} \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\mathbf{2} \hat{\mathbf{j}}-\mathbf{6} \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ to the position $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}_{\mathbf{2}}=\mathbf{1 4} \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\mathbf{1 3} \hat{\mathbf{j}}+\mathbf{9} \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ by a force $4 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+3 \hat{\mathbf{k}} \mathbf{N}$. If the work done by this force is $\mathbf{1 0} \mathbf{x}$ Joule, find the value of $\mathbf{x}$.
question: A particle is shifted from the position $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}_{\mathbf{1}}=\mathbf{3} \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\mathbf{2} \hat{\mathbf{j}}-\mathbf{6} \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ to the position $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{r}}_{\mathbf{2}}=\mathbf{1 4} \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\mathbf{1 3} \hat{\mathbf{j}}+\mathbf{9} \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ by a force $4 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+3 \hat{\mathbf{k}} \mathbf{N}$. If the work done by this force is $\mathbf{1 0} \mathbf{x}$ Joule, find the value of $\mathbf{x}$.

subject: Physics
q: A man of mass 50 kg carries a 20 kg load on his head and goes up 20 steps, each of height 0.25 m. If the least work done by the man on the block while climbing is 50 x Joule, find the value of x . (use $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
